content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates appropriate AoA-adjacent moves: refusing to strategize about the list, slowing the pace, redirecting from list to feeling, and inviting the client to let something 'just sit there' (turn 10) — which is in the neighborhood of meet-resistance-with-okay and feeling-the-unfelt-emotion. Turn 6's 'what happens if you blow Thursday?' is close to the false-end / 'what are you scared would happen' signature question, deployed at the right moment. However, the coach does not invoke signature framework language explicitly (no naming of what's-being-defended, no overwhelm-as-self-created reframe done skillfully, no explicit calibration of the threshold, no somatic invitation that opens the body). Turn 2's opening question ('is it fear, or exhaustion?') is closer to generic emotion-labeling than to a signature AoA move and risks pulling the client into figuring rather than feeling. The work is competent and directionally faithful, but it relies on general good-coaching instincts more than on the specific AoA toolkit — many of the signature questions and distinctions are adjacent rather than landed. Hits are present and appropriate, but few; this fits the 'few hits + all appropriate -> 2' cell of the matrix.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Generic emotion-labeling question ('fear or exhaustion?') rather than a signature AoA move; offers a multiple choice that invites figuring."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the move from feeling to list — refuses to strategize. Aligned with meet-resistance-with-okay in spirit, though not the signature 'what are you defending?' question."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What happens if you blow Thursday?' is a near-version of the false-end signature question, appropriately deployed."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Tracks the somatic/verbal interruption — close to the AoA move of noticing what's-not-being-said."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What if it got to just sit there...' — invites feeling-the-unfelt-emotion appropriately, though not in signature language."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What does it feel like, right now, to just let it be there?' — appropriate feel-over-figure move."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good Socratic question but a bit intellectual; misses the chance to deepen somatically into the heaviness the client just named."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "overwhelm"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-are-you-defending", "calibrate-the-threshold", "the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "Coach avoids the major traps (no list-strategizing, no 'you create your overwhelm,' no breath-to-repress), which is real fidelity. But signature questions and framework names are absent or only approximated; the coaching feels like good general feeling-oriented work rather than distinctively AoA."
}
